text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy town famous only for its old oak tree, lived a quirky old owl known far and wide for his wisdom. Every evening, he'd chirp tales that made the stars dance.

One particular autumn evening, the old owl opened his ancient eyes to hear a story about a moon gifted with feathers of glitter. It fluttered south each night singing sweet songs that could soothe hearts away from troubles.

The old owl loved that. He decided to share it himself. "Listen," he whispered. "Once, a fox decided he wished to experience true love. So each night he'd gaze upon the moon. But nothing happened."

The owl laughed. "And?"

"Well," said the fox dreamily. "Every night he flew higher."

"And?"

"The moon flew with him."

That night, stars appeared like wings.

From then on, love flew higher than ever. And every time their eyes met under the twinkling moonlit sky, hearts fluttered like winged
